title: Guardrails
content:
# Guardrails

Inherits from: ApiServiceModel


## Methods


### create_bulk_guardrails(data: Optional[Union[requests.BulkGuardrailsCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.BulkGuardrailsCreate

Create multiple guardrails at once.


### delete_bulk_guardrails(data: Optional[Union[requests.BulkGuardrailsDelete, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Deletes multiple guardrails.


### create_guardrail(data: Optional[Union[requests.GuardrailCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.GuardrailCreate

Create a new Guardrail.


### list_guardrails(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], search: Optional[Sequence[str]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.GuardrailsList, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[type.Guardrail], responses.GuardrailsListPaginatedResponseCursor]

List guardrails based on the provided filters.


### delete_guardrail(resource: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Delete a Guardrail by ID.


### get_guardrail(resource: str, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.GuardrailGet

Get a Guardrail by ID.


### update_guardrail(resource: str, data: Optional[Union[requests.GuardrailUpdate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> responses.GuardrailUpdate

Update a Guardrail by providing the Guardrail ID and the fields to update. This request changes the whole Guardrail.

from kelvin.api import Client
